source: Bergeaud & Verluise (2023), PLOS ONE — S2 Appendix annotation guidelines, keywords (S2.3.1) and CPC classes (S2.3.2) for Additive Manufacturing. The domain is defined at the FUNCTIONAL-APPLICATION level (a set of tasks), not by keyword presence.
definition: Additive Manufacturing: technology whose invention actually performs one of the defining Additive Manufacturing tasks (e.g. Create a 3D printable model with computer-aided design; Examine a stereolithography file for errors and inconsistency; Convert a model into a series of thin layers (slicing)). A patent is SEED only if its title+abstract clearly discusses doing one or more of the in-scope tasks below.

primary_decision_axis.name: performs_task_vs_mentions_or_adjacent
primary_decision_axis.rule: ACCEPT (SEED) only if the invention itself PERFORMS a Additive Manufacturing in-scope task with a concrete mechanism. REJECT (NOT_SEED) if it merely MENTIONS the domain, uses a related/adjacent technology for a DIFFERENT purpose, applies the domain's tools to another field, or belongs to a different technology entirely. Keyword presence alone is NOT sufficient — the functional task must be present.

in_scope_tasks.desc: Create a 3D printable model with computer-aided design
Examine a stereolithography file for errors and inconsistency
Convert a model into a series of thin layers (slicing)

out_of_scope_confusables: Patents from an ADJACENT/other Bergeaud domain (Self-driving Vehicle, Blockchain, Computer Vision, Genome Editing, Hydrogen Storage) that share vocabulary but do not perform a Additive Manufacturing task.
Generic patents that merely MENTION Additive Manufacturing terms without doing the task (e.g. listing it as background, prior art, or one possible application).
Using Additive Manufacturing-adjacent components/methods for a different end (no in-scope task).
hard_negative_patterns: domain keyword present but the invention does NOT perform a Additive Manufacturing task -> reject
the domain is named only as an example/application, the real contribution is elsewhere -> reject
an adjacent-domain invention that overlaps in terminology -> reject

score_anchors.0.9_1.0: the invention clearly PERFORMS a Additive Manufacturing in-scope task with a concrete mechanism
score_anchors.0.6_0.75: domain task plausibly implied but the mechanism/scope is unclear, or mixed signals -> boundary
score_anchors.0.0_0.25: merely mentions the domain, an adjacent-field/different-purpose use, or no domain signal at all
